summary.intent: Audit all 11 agnostic layout components from Epic #226 to verify spec coverage and implementation fidelity, then restructure the bloated layout test aggregates into per-component modules to match conventions used by other categories.
summary.outcome: All 11 layout components were audited, one real spec drift was fixed in `spec/components/layout/_category.md`, layout spec-conformance and proptest files were split into per-component directory-backed modules, a durable audit record was created at `docs/implementation/layout-category-audit.md`, branch coverage was brought above threshold with targeted Splitter tests, and all fast CI gate phases passed before committing.

repo: Layout tests were the last category still using flat aggregate files (`spec_conformance/layout.rs`, `proptest_state_machines/layout.rs`); all other categories (data-display, input, overlay, navigation, utility) already use directory-backed per-component modules.

The 11 agnostic layout components split into 5 stateless (AspectRatio, Center, Frame, Grid, Stack) and 6 stateful (Carousel, Collapsible, Portal, ScrollArea, Splitter, Toolbar); only stateful components have proptests.

`spec/components/layout/_category.md` Appendix D had a real spec drift: it pointed per-layout adapter feature flags at `ars-core/Cargo.toml` but those flags live in adapter crates, as documented in `spec/shared/layout-shared-types.md`.
`carousel/mod.rs` exceeds 100KB — full-file reads are impractical; targeted line-range reads and `rg` searches are required for that file.

The `cargo xci --profile fast` gate runs 10 sequential phases: fmt, clippy, unit/nextest (~9,100+ tests), i18n subsets, integration, adapter, spec snippets, error-variant coverage, mutual-exclusion/snapshot-count, and native llvm-cov coverage with per-crate branch thresholds.

GitHub project board item edits require resolving the project ID, item PVTI ID, field ID, and option ID via separate `gh project` list/field-list/item-list calls before running `gh project item-edit`.

code.finding: Collapse/expand helpers and panel/handle index guards had missed branch arms that put `ars-components` just below the 90% native branch coverage threshold; targeted unit tests for the no-op guard branches were sufficient to clear it.

Appendix D feature-flag table incorrectly attributed per-component layout feature flags to `ars-core`; the authoritative location is adapter crates, consistent with `spec/shared/layout-shared-types.md`.
Top-level test binary uses `mod layout;` to pull in the directory-backed layout module; the split required adding a `layout/mod.rs` and deleting the old `layout.rs` aggregate.

workflow: `rg` patterns that start with `--` (e.g., `--ars-`) are parsed as flags and fail; always use `-e <pattern>` for patterns beginning with dashes.

llvm-cov annotated text output is too large for direct inspection on a full workspace run; use `awk` to compute per-file BRF/BRH totals from the `.lcov` file to locate the highest-leverage missed branches.

`git diff --stat` only shows tracked modifications — newly created untracked split modules do not appear there; `git status --short` and `git ls-files --others` are needed to confirm new files are present.

The post-implementation audit skill mandates spec drift review followed by at least two 'anything else missing?' passes, then a coverage/test-surface phase — all findings must land in the same PR, not deferred issues.
For the project board update, `gh project item-list --jq` with a `.content.number == <N>` filter is the fastest way to isolate the right item ID from a large project.

friction: Initial worktree was in detached HEAD state, requiring an explicit `git switch -c` before any branch-tracked work could begin.
`rg` silently misinterpreted a pattern starting with `--ars-` as a flag, producing a confusing error that required re-running with `-e`.

The native branch coverage gate failed on the first `cargo xci --profile fast` run by a thin margin (ars-components just under 90%), requiring a second full fast-gate run after adding coverage tests — each run takes roughly 30–45 minutes.
The fast gate requires many sequential polling cycles with no structured wait mechanism, making the long-running coverage phase opaque until output arrives.
The llvm-cov `.lcov` file is workspace-wide and too large to inspect directly; a custom `awk` script was needed to surface per-file branch miss counts.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter component epics for the layout category are explicitly out of scope and remain as separate epics.
Epic #226 closure is contingent on the PR being merged, CI passing, and Codex review approval — the transcript ends at commit, so push/PR creation and final closure are still pending.
Stateless layout components were determined not to need proptests for this audit; if meaningful generated-input invariants are identified later, proptests for those components should be added.
